observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to make adjustments in the books and record infrastructure project paid out from trust fund at appropriate PPE account, and debit the account “Due to Other NGAs” only upon liquidation.  This is to ensure the correctness of the account balances in the books at year-end.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to demand the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers of their immediate submission of liquidation reports. Further, timely assess and monitor the project implementation of barangays and fairly present the assets of the municipality, by closing the account Due from LGUs in the year the funds are expended and require liquidation from the recipient barangays after the purpose of fund transfer is served, otherwise, additional fund transfer shall not be release.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the Receivable accounts and validate its existence and chances of collection. Effect appropriate measures in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 to process the writing-off of dormant accounts and consequently prevent the overstatement of the Receivable account in the year-end Financial Statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the evaluation of the status of implementation of the funds received from National Government Agencies, submit the liquidation of all implemented projects and remit the remaining balances thereof.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile the balance of the LDRRMF to be transfer to the Trust Fund Books and the LDRRMF actually transferred. Transfer the total unexpended balance of the LDRRMF-MOOE for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years pursuant to Par.5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of checks at least one month before a check becomes stale. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to establish the validity of the stale checks and effect appropriate adjustments in conformity with Section 59 of the NGAS Manual Volume I.

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/ activities are implemented and give due regard on its maximum utilization so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic Act 10121.

We recommend that management submit explanation for the delay in the implementation of projects under the 20% Development Fund. Henceforth, utilize all balances of the 20% Development Fund in CY 2021 in the implementation of development projects in order to attain the intended purpose for which the fund was appropriated.

We recommend that management maintain a special account in the General Fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities. Also separate financial statements has to be prepared and presented as part of the General
observations.managementAction: Management thru the Municipal Accountant complied with the Audit Recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management record the market building costing ₱ 20 M under the Building account as required under Section 112 of PD 1445 and compute the corresponding depreciation in accordance to COA Circular 2015-009 to accurately reflect the financial position of the agency at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The observation was well taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of supplies and materials using the perpetual inventory method pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Volume I Manual and the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit it to the Municipal Accountant which shall be used as basis in recording the issuances/expense account as required under Section 121 of the same manual. 

For the unutilized inventories at year end, instruct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to conduct a physical inventory and the Municipal Accountant to book the same as inventory in the books of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendation was well taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record transactions to its appropriate account as required in COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to accurately present the correct balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements. 

Henceforth, reclassify the entries made on the transactions which were inappropriately recorded as follows;

Other PPE	₱    154,320.50
   Prior Year’s Adjustments ₱154,320.50
observations.managementAction: The observation was well taken by management

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the transactions to its appropriate accounts as prescribed under COA Circular 2015-009 so as to reflect accurate account balances in the financial statements.

Henceforth, reclassify the entries as follows;

Other Land Improvement₱ 216,507.92
Other Supplies and Mat           7,480.00
Markets		         313,988.45
Prior Years Adjustment’s		₱537,076.37
observations.managementAction: Management was amenable to our observation and recommendation

observations.recommendation: We recommend management  instruct the Bids and Award Committee(BAC) to see to it that the documentary requirements for drugs and medicines be included in the eligibility requirements of supplier/s. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant has to  see to it that disbursement vouchers are supported with the documents required under Section .1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 well as the IRR of RA 11469 of related COVID19 medical supplies and see to it that all claims are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government funds are expended for valid, proper and legal purposes.
observations.managementAction: The observation was well taken by management

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the cash advances for senior citizen’s birthday gift as Advances to Special Disbursing Officer as provided in COA Circular 2015-009 in order to reflect accurate account balances in the financial statements.  

Also, reclassify the year-end balance as follows;

Advances to special Disbursing Officer   XXX
Due from Officers and Employees                      XXX
observations.managementAction: The observation was well taken by management.

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of drawing cash advance for senior citizen’s birthday gift in excess of the amount of payroll as required under COA Circular No. 97-002. The Municipal Accountant has to see to it that disbursement vouchers for its withdrawal be supported with a payroll showing the amount for the period and limit the drawing of cash advance to twice a month so as to avoid prolonged custody of cash in the hands of the Accountable Officer.
observations.managementAction: The observation was well taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management facilitate the registration of these lands in the name of the LGU under the Torrens Title System and safeguard it from squatters and unlawful occupants as required under Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2019

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables of said taxes pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to reflect in the book’s accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management see to it that acquisition of land has to be supported with complete documentary requirements as required in Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to substantiate the propriety and validity of the payment as well as to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management demand the liquidation of the financial assistance granted to LGUs upon expected completion of the project or the intended purpose has been served in accordance with existing regulations and to refrain from extending grant to recipients’ unliquidated financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Bids and Award Committee to require the submission by the winning bidder the complete set of technical specifications and the Municipal Accountant has to see to it that all contracts have to be supported with these documents as required under Section VII of the Philippine Bidding Documents to facilitate the timely technical review and evaluation of the price reasonableness and validity and propriety of payment is ascertained.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and submit the same to the auditor.

Furthermore, every time the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refueled at the refilling station, a checker/inspector have to accompany and check whether the correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into to vehicle or heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by management.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management particularly the Municipal Accountant admitted the audit exception and assured the team compliance in the ensuing year

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Agriculturist exert effort to verify the composition of this account and regularly monitor the changes of Breeding Stocks to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.
observations.managementAction: Management has instructed the Agriculture Office to verify the composition of these accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Agriculturist exert effort to verify the composition of this account and regularly monitor the changes of  Breeding Stocks to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.
observations.managementAction: Management has instructed the Agriculture Office to verify the composition of these accounts.
observations.reason: Review of the financial statements showed these are still carried in the books as such.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and submit the same to the auditor.

 Furthermore, every time the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refueled at the refilling station, a checker/inspector have to accompany and check whether the correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into to vehicle or heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: The drivers were instructed to fill up the Drivers Trip Tickets before going on a Trip.
observations.reason:
Post audit on transactions for payment of gasoline, oil and lubricants showed that these were supported with Driver’s Trip Ticket but is not the prescribed format and the necessary information were not indicated. 

Also the Monthly Report of Official Travel and Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle were not prepared. (Reiterated in 2019) 
The Driver’s Trip Tickets were not properly filled up. And the following information were still not indicated. A) Time of Departure and Arrival to and from the trip. B) Balance in tank before and after the trip. C) Quantity purchased. D) Odometer Reading E) Distance Travelled

Stop the practice of drawing cash advance for senior citizen’s birthday gift in excess of the amount of payroll as required under COA Circular No. 97-002. The Municipal Accountant has to see to it that disbursement vouchers for its withdrawal be supported with a payroll showing the amount for the period and limit the drawing of cash advance to twice a month so as to avoid prolonged custody of cash in the hands of the Accountable Officer.

We recommend that management instruct the Bids and Award Committee to require the submission by the winning bidder the complete set of technical specifications and the Municipal Accountant has to see to it that all contracts has to be supported with these documents as required under Section VII of the Philippine Bidding Documents to facilitate the timely technical review and evaluation of the price reasonableness and validity and propriety of payment is ascertained.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconciles the cashbooks and the accounting records at least quarterly to correct reconciling items in a timely manner and present an accurate account balance in accordance with Section 181 (c) of the Government (c) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I. 

Henceforth, require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to record/post transactions in their respective books promptly and regularly to facilitate the timely preparation and submission of reports as well as the reconciliation of monthly balances.
observations.managementAction: Previously, the reconciliation between accounting and treasurer were done on a monthly basis. After the exit conference they decided to do it on a weekly basis.
observations.reason: Tie up of accountability of the Municipal Treasurer during the cash examination showed that cashbook and general ledger balances for all cash accounts equal.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to comply with the documentary requirements for repairs, and maintain records for each property. Also, require the personnel concerned to subject the damaged parts of the vehicle/equipment to pre-repair inspection before requisition for replacement is made and to the replaced parts to post repair inspection by the designated LGU Inspector to ascertain that the vehicle/equipment is in operational condition and the parts requested were really installed. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all claims for repairs are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government funds are expended
observations.managementAction: The LCE instructed the conduct of the Pre and Post Repair Inspection before repairs are undertaken and paid.
observations.reason: Post audit of accounts showed that Disbursement vouchers for repairs were supported with Pre and Post Repair Inspection Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management evaluate the cause of the delay and personally charged the person/persons liable of the penalty instead of charging it against the funds of the government. It is also recommended that management see to it that loan payments are paid before due date to prevent the incurrence of penalties.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by management The Municipal Treasurer assured the audit team compliance to the recommendation in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Review of the loan payment to MDFO showed that it is paid within the time frame or on or before the due date and only the amount billed is paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s  Loboc Tourism Complex and Loboc Eco Tourism Adventure Park.
observations.managementAction: Management has made representations with the BIR and has applied for Registration.
observations.reason: Inquiry with the Municipal Accountant revealed that the processing of their application for Registration with BIR was delayed due to the pandemic

observations.reason: Review  of  the financial statements showed that Reciprocal Accounts Due From Other Funds and Due to Other Funds showed a difference of ₱12,051,339.53 is reduced to ₱ 19,841.74.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables of said taxes pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The concerned office is still verifying the accurate figure to be used as the basis of the accrual since it is actually the first time the LGU will be implementing. Although the actual figure is yet to be determined, steps are already undertaken to ensure compliance with the audit recommendation.
observations.reason: Management thru the Municipal Treasurer informed the audit team that she was not able to submit the Certified List of Taxpayers based on the RPTAR due to its non-availability. The RPTARS were destroyed during the flood that hit the municipality in 2014. Reconstruction of these records will take time considering the various concerns of her office that needs her immediate attention and the lack of personnel to do so. Thus, they relied on the Quarterly Report of Real Property Assessment from the Office of the Municipal Assessor as basis of the amount of RPT and SET Receivables being set up by the Municipal Accountant.

We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team  to immediately prepare and  submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with  Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1.  Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer  and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted a physical count of the municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment witnessed by the Audit Team on December, 2018. The corresponding RPCPPE was submitted to the Audit Team on February 5, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the 

Municipal Accountant to properly coordinate to have all the expenditures obligated and recorded in the year they were incurred.  Unpaid obligations/expenses incurred during the year have to be recorded as Accounts Payable in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statement at year end.
observations.managementAction: Management admitted the exception and the Office of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer coordinated to have all the expenditures obligated on the year incurred.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the fund transfers to its appropriate account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009  in order to accurately present the correct balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management particularly the Municipal Accountant admitted the exception and assured the audit team of compliance in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management appropriate at least 5% from the total estimated income from regular sources for Calamity Fund as provided in Section 1 of Rule 18 RA 10121. Observe strictly the proper allocation of the fund as to 70% for  Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund LDRRMF and 30% as Quick Response Fund (QRE). Give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose  is attained/achieved as provided in Section 2 
and 3 of Rule 18 RA 10121.
observations.managementAction: The observation and recommendation are well taken by management The Municipal Budget Officer assured the audit team compliance to the recommendation in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management demand from the contractor  the amount of  liquidated damages for contracts which are not completed within the contract time as provided in Section 68 of the IRR of Republic Act 9184. It is also recommended that the concerned municipal officials properly monitor the implementation of projects to void delay and ensure its completion
observations.managementAction: The LGU is undergoing  review of all the contracts including the  necessary documents such as approved work extensions, extra work and suspension orders to support the imposition of liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management avoid paying honoraria to salaried officials and employees who are designated to act on related services in the LGUs operations and implementation of its program and projects.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the grant of honorarium to the Administrative Aide was due to his responsibility as LEAP  Manager. His salary is not commensurate of his responsibility  so they opted to grant him honorarium. However they agreed to comply our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the MSWDO to maintain a cashbook as required under Section 181 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Volume I to facilitate the monitoring of cash advances at a given time.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO was instructed to maintain an official cashbook to record her cash advances as well as liquidations thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the property custodian of the municipality to have all motor vehicles owned by the municipal government, except security vehicle, marked “For Official Use Only”, under which shall be written the information required under COA Circular no. 77-61. Furthermore, see to it that all government vehicles be registered and issued with the corresponding government plate.
observations.managementAction: The property custodian agreed to implement the recommendation. The required marking of “For Official Use Only”  for each vehicle and the use of red plates.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team  to immediately conduct  the physical inventory of all properties and  submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with  Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1. Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer  and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted a physical count of the municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment witnessed by the Audit Team on December, 2018. The corresponding RPCPPE was submitted to the Audit Team on February 5, 2020.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal officials concerned to adhere to the requirements of COA Circular 98-002 in the hiring of private lawyer as their legal counsel. They have to exhaust all  means to seek assistance from the Provincial Legal Office to handle their legal cases.  In the event that a government lawyer is not available, a written conformity from the Solicitor General with concurrence from the Commission on Audit have to be first secured before hiring the services of  private lawyer.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has stopped hiring the services of  Atty. Carlos Cesar Castillo pending compliance to the requirements of COA Circular No. 98-002.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate cancellation/adjustment of the stale checks giving priority to long outstanding prior years issuance in order to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the  fair presentation of the financial statements at year end.

 Also, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement/s and to regularly submit the same every month in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply with the requirements set forth under Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of COA, DBM. DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND.  Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the COA ATL the disbursement voucher pertaining to the cash advance for Confidential Fund including all the supporting documents thereof for review as to completeness of the documentary requirements, pursuant to Section 6.3.3 (d) of the Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in order to ascertain the propriety of the cash advance and facilitate the submission to the ICFAU of the report on the results of the audit thereon.
observations.managementAction: The former local Chief Executive was not allowed to draw cash advance for Confidential Expenses for the period January 2016 to June 2019.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.
observations.managementAction: Management particularly the Municipal Accountant admitted the audit exception and assured the team compliance in the ensuing year

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables of said taxes pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables.
observations.managementAction: The concerned office  is still verifying the accurate figure to be used as the basis of the accrual since it is actually the first time the LGU will be implementing. Although the actual figure is yet to be determined, steps are already undertaken to ensure compliance with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the immediate cancellation/adjustment of the stale checks giving priority to long outstanding prior years issuance in order to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the  fair presentation of the financial statements at year end.

     Also, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement/s and to regularly submit the same every month in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued an office order  which would address the issue. His office made an assessment that accuracy of the entries must prevail over anything else for fair presentation of financial statements. He acknowledge the need to verify the transactions before a reversing entry is made. For transactions prior to 2014, an in depth retrieval of all available data before the entry will be made.
observations.reason:
Review of the bank reconciliation statements as of December 31, 2018 showed that the above stale checks remained as reconciling items. This indicated that the Municipal Accountant did not implement our recommendation as he felt the need to verify the transactions before adjusting the entries of these stale checks.

Moreover, Bank Reconciliation Statements were not prepared and submitted by the Municipal Accountant to the Auditor as required under existing regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly comply with the requirements set forth under Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of COA, DBM. DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND.  Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the COA ATL the disbursement voucher pertaining to the cash advance for Confidential Fund including all the supporting documents thereof for review as to completeness of the documentary requirements, pursuant to Section 6.3.3 (d) of the Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in order to ascertain the propriety of the cash advance and facilitate the submission to the ICFAU of the report on the results of the audit thereon.
observations.managementAction: Management has not complied on this recommendation due to time and manpower constraint. For the CY 2017 and 2018 the LCE was unable to draw/utilize the Confidential Fund.
observations.reason: Review of the Checks Disbursement  Journals-General Fund for CY 2017 and 2018 showed no issuance of check for cash advance on Confidential Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team  to immediately prepare and  submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with  Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1.  Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer  and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to properly coordinate to have all the expenditures obligated and recorded in the year they were incurred.  Unpaid obligations/expenses incurred during the year have to be recorded as Accounts Payable in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statement at year end.

It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables of said taxes pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the fund transfers to its appropriate account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009  in order to accurately present the correct balances of the accounts in the year-end financial statements.

We recommend that Management appropriate at least 5% from the total estimated income from regular sources for Calamity Fund as provided in Section 1 of Rule 18 RA 10121. Observe strictly the proper allocation of the fund as to 70% for  Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund LDRRMF and 30% as Quick Response Fund (QRE). Give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that  intended purpose  is attained/achieved as provided in Section 2 and 3 of Rule 18 RA 10121.

We recommend that management demand from the contractor  the amount of  liquidated damages for contracts which are not completed within the contract time as provided in Section 68 of the IRR of Republic Act 9184. It is also recommended that the concerned municipal officials properly monitor the implementation of projects to void delay and ensure its completion.

We recommend that Management avoid paying honoraria to salaried officials and employees who are designated to act on related services in the LGUs  operations and  implementation of its program and projects.

We recommend that management require the MSWDO to maintain a cashbook as required under Section 181 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Volume I to facilitate the monitoring of cash advances at a given time.

We recommend that management require the property custodian of the municipality to have all motor vehicles owned by the municipal government, except security vehicle, be marked “For Official Use Only”, under which shall be written the information required under COA Circular no. 77-61. Furthermore, see to it that all government vehicles be registered and issued with the corresponding government plate.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to properly coordinate to have all the expenditures obligated and recorded in the year they were incurred.  Unpaid obligations/expenses incurred during the year has to be recorded as Accounts Payable in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statement at year end.
observations.managementAction: Management already implemented the recommendation in 2018.
observations.reason: Post audit of accounts and review of  the General Ledger showed that unpaid expenses/obligations during the year were recorded as Accounts Payable  as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate cashbook for cash advances granted for wages in the Special Education and Trust Fund and to record the corresponding payments out of the cash advance in the same cashbook in accordance with Section 28 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has complied the recommendation.
observations.reason: The cashbook was already maintained by the Municipal Treasurer in which the Advances for wages of teachers in the Special Education Fund and the corresponding payments are recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to take up the grant of the cash advance as a debit to the asset account Payroll Fund and a credit to the account Cash in Bank, Local Currency, Current Account and to record the liquidations made by a debit to the expense account Wages and a credit to the asset account Payroll Fund in the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2004-008.  Henceforth, strict adherence to the proper accounting treatment in the grant and liquidation of cash advances is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has complied the recommendation.
observations.reason: Verification of the Check Disbursement Journals and General Ledger showed that Payroll Fund account is debited upon drawing of the check for cash advance and is credited upon payment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management adhere strictly to the provisions on Section 7.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations(IRR) of R. A. No. 9184 in the procurement of consultancy services and comply with the requirements on Section 7.3.2 of the same  IRR .

Evaluate further the need of  consultant viz-a-viz their respective expertise and based on the result of such review, consider the termination of the contracts when services provided are redundant and unnecessary.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the recommendation. Procurement of consulting and other services were already included in the Annual Procurement Plan(APP) and if there are procurement which are not included in the APP, a revision of the original plan is made before the procurement.
observations.reason: Post audit of  disbursement particularly consultancy  services showed substantial compliance by management in their procurement of consulting services.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management  comply with the documentary requirements for repairs, and maintain records for each property. Also, require the /personnel concerned to subject the damaged parts of the vehicle/equipment to pre-repair inspection before requisition for replacement is made and to the replaced parts to post repair inspection by the designated LGU Inspector to ascertain that the vehicle/equipment  is in operational condition and the parts requested were really installed. Furthermore,  require the Municipal Accountant to  see to it that all claims for repairs  are supported with complete documentation to ensure that government funds are expended for  proper and valid expenses as required under existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management complied on the documentary requirements for claims of  repairs and maintenance as required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.reason: Post audit of disbursement particularly on repairs and maintenance showed management’s compliance on the documentary requirements particularly on the Pre and Post Repair  Inspection Report as well the Report of Waste Material.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipality establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children and allocate 1% of its IRA for the programs. projects and activities for the protection of children and youth in accordance with the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2002-121 and Section 15 of Republic Act 9344
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued an office order creating the LCPC and included in the 2018 Annual Investment Plan the minimum required budget allocation the mandated 1 % of IRA.
observations.reason: Review of the municipality’s Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget showed that PAPS are already streamlined in the various offices particularly GAD and DSWD in compliance to RA 9344.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct  the Municipal Accountant to record the collections/ income of the LGU to its appropriate income account as required under Section 19 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Recording of income were already credited to its appropriate account
observations.reason: Verification with  the General Ledger and Trial Balance showed that the account Other Deferred Credits is eliminated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountable officer be bonded based on the bonding requirement provided for under Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to prevent the possible risk in the recovery of government funds in case of loss or misappropriation
observations.managementAction:
The Fidelity  Bond of the Disbursing Officer is increased from 
₱60,000.00 to ₱225,000.00 effective March 23, 2018.
observations.reason: Verification of the  Confirmation Letter from the Bureau of Treasury showed that Ms. Cristina M. Tejada, the Assistant Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer of the LGU has been increased to ₱225,000.00 effective March 23, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team  to immediately conduct  the physical inventory of all properties and  submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with  Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1. Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer  and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Management complied the recommendation. The Inventory Committee conducted a physical count of its properties in December of 2018 but was unable to submit to the Auditor  the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment(RPCPPE)
observations.reason: The Inventory  Committee conducted a physical count of its properties witnessed by the Audit Team but was not able to submit the RPCPPE to the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year as required under Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs  due to manpower and  time constraint. (Reiterated as Finding No. 1 of AAR 2018)

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal officials concerned to adhere to the requirements of COA Circular 98-002 in the hiring of private lawyer as their legal counsel. They have to exhaust all  means to seek assistance from the Provincial Legal Office to handle their legal cases.  In the event that a government lawyer is not available, a written conformity from the Solicitor General with concurrence from the Commission on Audit have to be first secured before hiring the services of  private lawyer.
observations.managementAction: Management is still in the process of complying requirements in the hiring of private lawyer as their legal counsel particularly the written conformity from the Solicitor General and concurrence from the Commission on Audit (COA) which can only filed in COA Central Office
observations.reason: Post audit of disbursement particularly legal fees showed that payment for legal fees to Atty. Carlos Cesar Castillo were made up to July of 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption Report for each vehicle and  submit the same to the auditor.

 Furthermore, everytime the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refueled at the refilling station, a checker/inspector have to accompany and check whether the correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into to vehicle or heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: Payment for fuel are supported with Driver’s Trip Tickets and Statement of Account from the gasoline station.
observations.reason: Post audit of disbursement on fuel, oil and lubricant showed that these were supported with a Driver’s Trip Ticket but not in the prescribed format/form. The necessary information such as the odometer reading, distance travelled, balance in tank, fuel purchased and used during the trip were not indicated. Also, the Fuel Consumption Report and Monthly Report of Official Travel for each vehicle were not prepared and submitted to the Auditor as required under COA Circular No 77-61

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
effect the immediate cancellation/adjustment of the stale checks giving priority to
long outstanding prior years issuance in order to reflect the correct balance of the
asset account and the fair presentation of the financial statements at year end.
Also, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statement/s and to regularly submit the same every month in the
ensuing year.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and
the Municipal Accountant to properly coordinate to have all the expenditures
obligated and recorded in the year they were incurred. Unpaid
obligations/expenses incurred during the year has to be recorded as Accounts
Payable in order to have a fair presentation of the financial statement at year end.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
maintain a separate cashbook for cash advances granted for wages in the Special
Education and Trust Fund and to record the corresponding payments out of the
cash advance in the same cashbook in accordance with Section 28 of NGAS Manual
for LGUs Volume II.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to take up the grant of the cash
advance as a debit to the asset account Payroll Fund and a credit to the account
Cash in Bank, Local Currency, Current Account and to record the liquidations
made by a debit to the expense account Wages and a credit to the asset account
Payroll Fund in the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2004-
008. Henceforth, strict adherence to the proper accounting treatment in the grant
and liquidation of cash advances is enjoined.

We recommend that management adhere strictly to the provisions on Section
7.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations(IRR) of R. A. No. 9184 in
the procurement of consultancy services and comply with the requirements on
Section 7.3.2 of the same IRR .
Evaluate further the need of consultant viz-a-viz their respective expertise
and based on the result of such review, consider the termination of the contracts
when services provided are redundant and unnecessary.

Require the municipal officials concerned to adhere to the requirements of
COA Circular 98-002 in the hiring of private lawyer as their legal counsel. They
have to exhaust all means to seek assistance from the Provincial Legal Office to
handle their legal cases. In the event that a government lawyer is not available, a
written conformity from the Solicitor General with concurrence from the
Commission on Audit have to be first secured before hiring the services of private
lawyer.

We recommend that management require all officials and employees
concerned and responsible in the preparation, approving of trip tickets as well as
authorized passenger/s to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly
accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip. Also, require the
preparation of the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Fuel Consumption
Report for each vehicle and submit the same to the auditor.
Furthermore, everytime the vehicle/heavy equipment shall be refueled at the
refilling station, a checker/inspector have to accompany and check whether the
correct quantity of fuel being issued is the same as that pumped into to vehicle or
heavy equipment.

We recommend management comply with the documentary requirements
for repairs, and maintain records for each property. Also, require the /personnel
concerned to subject the damaged parts of the vehicle/equipment to pre-repair
inspection before requisition for replacement is made and to the replaced parts to
post repair inspection by the designated LGU Inspector to ascertain that the
vehicle/equipment is in operational condition and the parts requested were really
installed. Furthermore, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all
claims for repairs are supported with complete documentation to ensure that
government funds are expended for proper and valid expenses as required under
existing regulations.

We recommend that the Municipality establish the Local Council for the
Protection of Children and allocate 1% of its IRA for the programs. projects and
activities for the protection of children and youth in accordance with the DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2002-121 and Section 15 of Republic Act 9344.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry
Voucher(JEV) in all municipal transactions in which the accounting entries are
reflected, as basis for its recording in the appropriate journals as required under
Section 31 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommend that the accountable officer be only bonded based on the bonding requirement provided for under Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to prevent the possible risk in the recovery of government funds in case of loss or misappropriation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is still in consultation with the Bureau of the Treasury as to the increase of the bond of the Disbursing Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement the collection of ₱100.00 from every paying guest who avails the Floating Restaurant River Cruise services at the Loboc Tourism Center (LTC) in accordance with Municipal Ordinance No. 01-series of 2010 in order to attain the collection targets of the LTC during the particular period.
observations.managementAction: The economic planners of the tourism enterprise saw it necessary to implement Resolution No. 132-2014 and Ordinance No. 01-series of 2010 in order to attain collection targets of the LTC during the particular period.
observations.reason: The economic planners of the tourism enterprise saw it necessary to implement Resolution No. 132-2014 to boost tourism given the decline in tourist arrivals after the earthquake. (Reiterated as finding No. 8 of AAR 2016)

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team to
immediately conduct the physical inventory of all properties and submit the
corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to
the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government
Accounting System (NGAS), Volume1. Henceforth, require the reconciliation of
records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant with the
Property/Equipment Card maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer
and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

We recommend to management to require the Municipal Accountant to
provide a residual value of the depreciable assets and recompute the depreciation as
well as the accumulated depreciation and effect the necessary adjustment to have a
fair presentation of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that management designate a limited number of personnel
who shall act as special disbursing Officer of the LGU who shall be adequately
bonded in accordance with law and at all times shall be subject to audit of their
respective cash accountabilities in order to safeguard government funds against
misappropriation or unauthorized use for other purposes to protect the interest of
the government.

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the
LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard
on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that
intended purpose is attained/achieved.

We recommend that management appoint officials and personnel to conduct
DRRM activities to assure that risk of lives and property be reduced in times of
calamity and necessary and adequate disaster preparedness could be undertaken.
Further, we also recommend that MDRRMO be strengthened for them to be
responsive in times of calamities.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to
record the collections/ income of the LGU to its appropriate income account as
required under Section 19 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I.

We recommend that management revisit Municipal Ordinance No. 04,
Series of 2015 and properly evaluate the impact of its continued implementation.
Instruct the economic planners to exert extra efforts to find alternative strategies
to sustain the tourist arrival aside from giving monetary incentives to van drivers
for bringing in guest to the LTC.

We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and submit the monthly trial balances and financial statements to the
Office of the Auditor as required under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs
Volume 1 and to maintain subsidiary ledgers for special accounts pursuant to
Sections 105 and 107 of the same manual to facilitate the preparation and prompt
submission of the year end financial reports of the LGU.

We recommend that Management limit the grant of the cash advance for Confidential Fund equivalent to the three-month cash requirements for the total budget of P700,000.00, pursuant to COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 and COA Circular No. 2012-003. See to it that the grant of cash advances is equally allocated on a quarterly basis during the year based on the Physical and Financial Plan in order to serve the purpose for which the funds were appropriated of providing the necessary cash requirement for the purchase of intelligence information and other confidential operations and facilitate the quarterly submission of the Liquidation Reports of the cash advances for Confidential Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Mgt. of LGU-Loboc will ensure compliance of Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Designated Solid Waste Management Focal Person and the concerned LGU officials to accelerate the procurement of the garbage compactor in the ensuing year in order to fully realize its goals on solid waste management geared towards public health and environmental protection. Further direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to review and update the projects, programs and activities on solid waste management to be implemented by the LGU as required under Section 16 of R. A. 9003 and require the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate the necessary funds for its implementation in order to ensure its sustainability, viability and relevance to solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: Purchase of garbage compactor was realized in the 3rd quarter of 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management: maximize the utilization of 70% of appropriations to support pre-disaster risk management activities during the year; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to transfer the unexpended LDRRM appropriations of the 2015 LDRRMF and those of prior years’ LDRRMF to a special trust fund in the Trust Fund intended solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years.
observations.managementAction: Managemen t has made a Detailed and Comprehens ive Financial Plan relative to the development budget. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to transfer the unexpended LDRRM appropriations of the 2015 LDRRMF and those of prior years’ LDRRMF to a special trust fund in the Trust Fund intended solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years.
observations.reason: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to transfer the unexpended LDRRM appropriations of the 2015 LDRRMF and those of prior years’ LDRRMF to a special trust fund in the Trust Fund intended solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five (5) years.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management maximize the utilization of the appropriations for Gender and Development (GAD) to afford full implementation of the identified Programs, Activities and Projects (PAPs) in the GAD Plan. Prioritize the implementation of the PAPs that directly address gender-related issues and concerns as identified in the PPGD for 1995-2025 and those identified and authorized under specific laws for gender and development. Further, instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and furnish the COA Audit Team with a copy of the GAD Plan and Budget within five (5) working days from receipt of the approved GAD Plan from the Philippine Commission for Women or the DILG, as the case may be, and a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year for audit reference in the review of GAD Programs, Activities and Projects planned and implemented by the LGU during the year.
observations.managementAction: The Partially Implemented. Prioritize the implementation of the PAPs that directly address gender-related issues and concerns. Instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and furnish the COA Audit Team with a copy of the GAD Plan and Budget within five (5) working days from receipt of the approved GAD Plan from the Philippine Commission for Women or the DILG, as the case may be, and a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year for audit reference in the review of GAD Programs, Activities and Projects planned and implemented by the LGU during the year.
observations.reason: The charging of expenses to GAD were made on the belief that these activities address the GAD related issues and concerns and these would directly and indirectly benefit constituents particularly women and children.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management advice the Local School Board to make representations with the Sangguniang Bayan for the possible appropriation of funds in the General Fund for expenses necessary for the implementation of the identified programs and activities and to stop the practice of charging expenses not aligned with the specific purpose of the SEF and require the Local School Board to adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of R. A. 7160 and Section 83 of Volume I of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs in identifying the programs and activities to be funded out of the SEF.
observations.managementAction: Charging of salaries and security is silent regarding the applicability of the items subject to SEF utilization. However with the issuance of Joint Circular 2017 everything is clear.
observations.reason: The guidelines as to SEF utilization is silent regarding the applicability of the items subject to SEF utilization. However with the issuance of Joint Circular 2017 everything is clear.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU abide with the provisions of Sections 85 to 87 of P. D. 1445 and Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, Series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007 of the DILG, NEDA, DBM and DOF to prevent future disallowances of these transactions. In view of the Supreme Court’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that all appropriations shall be incorporated in the General Annual Investment Plan and Project Procurement Management Plan of the LGU and implemented by the concerned department or office.
observations.managementAction: The generic appropriation is not anymore included in the succeeding year

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of contracts for infrastructure projects and purchase orders for goods, together with its integral and supporting parts/supporting documents, within five (5) days from perfection of the contracts to enable the Audit Team to conduct the timely auditorial/legal review and the ocular inspection on the deliveries of goods at the most appropriate time and manner. Further, instruct the Inspection Officer to furnish the Audit Team with copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts, within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the latter to conduct the timely inspection thereof, at the most appropriate time and manner, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has designated an Assistant Secretary to the BAC who will take charge with the BAC Secretariat. The voluminous transactions and BAC related documents coupled on the resignation of some members of the BAC and the Assistant Secretary who will take charge with the BAC Secretariat, the submission of contracts and Purchase Orders and documents supporting the contracts were set aside. Further, instruct the Inspection Officer to furnish the Audit Team with copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts, within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the latter to conduct the timely inspection thereof, at the most appropriate time and manner, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.
observations.reason: Due to time and manpower constraint. The voluminous transactions and BAC related documents coupled with the resignation of some members of the BAC and the Assistant Secretary the submission of the contracts and Purchase Orders and documents supporting the contracts were set aside.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit to the COA ATL the disbursement voucher pertaining to the cash advance for Confidential Fund including all the supporting documents thereof for review as to completeness of the documentary requirements, pursuant to Section 6.3.3 (d) of the Joint Circular No. 2015-01 in order to ascertain the propriety of the cash advance and facilitate the submission to the ICFAU of the report on the results of the audit thereon.
observations.managementAction: Implement the requirement to submit the disbursement voucher pertaining to the cash advance for Confidential Fund including all the supporting documents for review as to completeness of documentary requirements.
observations.reason: The issue is still being assessed by the Bureau of the Treasury as to the increase of the bond of the disbursing officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the collections of the Special Education Fund directly to the SEF depository account intact and regularly in accordance with Sections 13 and 32 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs in order to properly account for all SEF collections during the year and accrue the interest income for the fund and at the same time ascertain the cash accountability of the Municipal Treasurer for the SEF at any given date or period.
observations.managementAction: SEF collections were already deposited to the SEF depository bank account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer record the transactions pertaining to collections, deposits and withdrawals of the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund in the prescribed cashbook by the prescribed cashbook in accordance with Sections 26 and 27 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs and to record the grant of cash advance as a debit to the asset account Payroll Fund and a credit to the account Cash in Bank, Local Currency, Current Account and to record the liquidations made by a debit to the expense account Wages and a credit to the asset account Payroll Fund in the books of accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2004-008. Henceforth, strict adherence to the proper accounting treatment in the grant and liquidation of cash advances is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has instructed staff to record collections, deposits and withdrawals on prescribed cashbooks. The practice will be fully implemented in 2017.
observations.reason: It has been the practice of the LGU for quite a period of time and their attention was not called. This will be fully implemented in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer immediately stop the practice of paying check disbursements from the General Fund current account in order to properly account for all check disbursements of the SEF during the year and at the same time ascertain the correct bank account balances of the SEF and the General Fund at any given date or period. Further, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exercise prudence in the use of blank checks to preclude any error in the payment of claims in the SEF and GF. Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all check disbursements to the appropriate fund in accordance with Section 43 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to avoid ambiguity in the recording of check disbursements and the account balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has exercised care in the issuance of checks of the SEF and GF and ensured proper accounting thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer record the transactions pertaining to collections, deposits and withdrawals of the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund in the prescribed cashbook by prescribed cashbook pursuant to Sections 26 and 27 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs in order to keep her official cash records intact and thus preclude the loss or pilferage thereof.
observations.managementAction: Recording of collections, deposits and withdrawals were already made to the hardbound cashbook based on her computerized entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of paying the full amount of RATA on the first week of the month before services were rendered. Management can either pay the proportionate amount in relation to the number of days work or to pay the full amount after 17 working days in accordance with Section 7.1 of DBM LBC 103.
observations.managementAction: Payment of RATA were made on 3rd week of the month.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate on the implementation of priority under the 20% development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% development Fund and during the year the funds were actually appropriated to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcomes from the said programs or projects. Henceforth, refrain from appropriating funds for PPAs not properly chargeable against the 20% Development Fund to avoid disallowance in audit of the transactions involved.
observations.managementAction: Managemen t had not fully implemente d the identified projects due to influx of BEA and BUB funds that are quite material and the needs of development projects were prioritized.
observations.reason: Due to the influx of BEA and BUB funds that are quite material and the needs of development projects that required prompt attention, projects were prioritized and implemented.
